1. To calculate the probability that a randomly selected student from Group B has a higher engagement level than a randomly selected student from Group A, we need to find the probability that $X_B > X_A$. This is equivalent to finding $P(X_B > X_A)$.

Given that $X_A \sim N(70, 10^2)$ and $X_B \sim N(75, 12^2)$, the difference $X_B - X_A$ follows a normal distribution with mean $\mu = \mu_B - \mu_A = 75 - 70 = 5$ and variance $\sigma^2 = \sigma_B^2 + \sigma_A^2 = 12^2 + 10^2 = 144 + 100 = 244$. Thus, $X_B - X_A \sim N(5, 244)$.

We want to find $P(X_B > X_A) = P(X_B - X_A > 0)$. This is equivalent to finding $P(Z > -5/√244)$ where $Z$ is a standard normal variable.

The standard deviation of $X_B - X_A$ is $\sqrt{244} \approx 15.62$. So, we have:

$P(Z > -5/15.62) = P(Z > -0.319)$. Using standard normal distribution tables or a calculator, this probability is approximately 0.624.

Therefore, the probability that a randomly selected student from Group B has a higher engagement level than a randomly selected student from Group A is approximately 0.624.
